prose.title: 91p mos
prose.body:
91P MOS is the acronym for Military Occupational Specialty: Prime Power Production Specialist.

The Prime Power Production Specialist is a crucial role in the Army Corps of Engineers. They are responsible for operating and maintaining electrical power generating plants and provide assistance in planning, coordinating, and executing equipment maintenance operations.

Some of the specific duties of a 91P MOS may include:

- Operating and maintaining power equipment, including generators, engines, and electrical systems
- Conducting preventative maintenance checks and services on power generation equipment
- Troubleshooting and repairing mechanical and electrical failures in power generation systems
- Assisting with planning and implementing power generation operations in various environments and conditions
- Ensuring compliance with safety protocols and regulations

To become a Prime Power Production Specialist (91P), an individual must complete Basic Combat Training and Advanced Individual Training (AIT) at Fort Leonard Wood, Missouri. AIT for 91P lasts approximately 16 weeks, and it provides hands-on training on power generation systems, electrical theory, and maintenance procedures.

After completing AIT, 91P soldiers can work in a wide range of environments, both stateside and overseas. They may support various mission requirements, including disaster relief operations, deployment assignments, and construction projects.

It is important to note that specific job assignments within the 91P MOS can vary based on the needs of the Army and the soldier's skills and qualifications.
